commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(worker): resolve Jackson version conflict with OpenSearch client (#467)

The opensearch-rest-high-level-client 2.17.1 requires Jackson 2.17.x,
but Spring Boot 3.2.2 pins Jackson to 2.15.3. This causes
NoClassDefFoundError for StreamWriteConstraints when the OpenSearch
client tries to serialize documents, crashing every request that passes
through LoginTrackingFilter.

- Override jackson-bom.version to 2.17.2 in worker pom.xml
- Change LoginTrackingFilter catch clause from Exception to Throwable
  to handle NoClassDefFoundError and ExceptionInInitializerError
  gracefully, preventing audit failures from crashing normal requests

Co-authored-by: Craig Klinker <craigklinker@velocityglobal.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: sync PROGRESS.md + PENDING-TASKS.md — step 9 complete [30]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(mac): AppArmor profile + SELinux label support (#63, #64)

Implements exec-time MAC (Mandatory Access Control) profile application
for both AppArmor (#63) and SELinux (#64) via the Linux LSM procfs attr
interface, following runc's two-step fd technique.

## Design

**Two-step fd technique** (same as runc/Docker):
1. Open /proc/self/attr/apparmor/exec (or /proc/self/attr/exec for SELinux)
   *before* chroot/pivot_root (pre_exec step 3.9) while the host's /proc
   is still accessible.  O_CLOEXEC closes the fd at exec if unused.
2. Write the profile/label name *after* all security setup (caps dropped,
   NNP set, Landlock applied) but *before* seccomp (pre_exec step 6.56),
   so no seccomp filter can block the write(2) call.

**Graceful degradation**: LSM availability is checked in the *parent*
process (before fork) using is_apparmor_enabled() / is_selinux_enabled(),
which can safely allocate.  If the LSM is not running the profile/label
field is cleared and the fd-open is skipped entirely — no EINVAL, no
crash.  A non-zero write() result (e.g. ENOENT for unknown profile) is
propagated as a spawn error.

Both spawn paths (root + rootless) are updated identically.

## New API

- `Command::with_apparmor_profile(profile: &str)` — apply named profile at exec
- `Command::with_selinux_label(label: &str)` — apply SELinux label at exec
- `--apparmor-profile PROFILE` and `--selinux-label LABEL` on `pelagos run`
- `(apparmor-profile "name")` and `(selinux-label "u:r:t:s")` in compose specs
- `pelagos::mac::is_apparmor_enabled()` and `is_selinux_enabled()` for introspection

## New files

- `src/mac.rs` — MAC helper module
- `scripts/apparmor-profiles/pelagos-container` — production AppArmor profile
  template (restrictive baseline matching DEFAULT_CAPS, documented for customisation)
- `scripts/apparmor-profiles/pelagos-test` — fully permissive profile for tests

## Tests (3 new, all pass)

- `mac::test_apparmor_profile_unconfined` — smoke: "unconfined" profile, exit 0
- `mac::test_apparmor_profile_applied` — load pelagos-test profile, verify
  /proc/self/attr/current shows it; skips when AppArmor disabled or parser absent
- `mac::test_selinux_label_no_selinux` — always runs; asserts graceful skip when
  SELinux absent; guards the degradation path on production hosts

246/246 integration tests pass, 6 ignored.  296/296 unit tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add comprehensive E2E test suite: 31 P1 + 21 P2 cases (52 total)

P1 (critical path):
- Session creation (basic, all options, duplicate rejection)
- Recurring sessions (parent + children)
- Attendee join by code
- Question submission (anonymous, named, bulk)
- Upvote/un-upvote + sort order
- Host & attendee replies
- Mark/unmark answered
- Moderation flow (enable, submit unapproved, approve)
- Cluster CRUD + assign questions
- Highlight cluster (discussing now)
- Session lifecycle (end, reopen)
- FAQ library save
- Input validation (500 char limit, 501 rejected, special chars)
- Data integrity (cross-table relationships)

P2 (important):
- Multiple replies + ordering
- Reject question, disable moderation
- Cluster claim/unclaim, stop highlighting
- Analytics queries (counts, answer rate, upvotes)
- Presenter view data (clusters + unclustered questions)
- Report/export data joins
- Concurrent operations (10 parallel inserts, concurrent upvotes)
- Auto-suggest toggle
- Empty text detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify WaitForkInAlwaysCombErrors → §9.2.2.2.2 [skip ci]

§9.2.2.2.2 ("always_comb compared to always @*") explicitly prohibits statements that block inside always_comb. wait fork is a blocking statement by definition (§9.6.1 defines it as suspending the process until child subprocesses terminate), so it falls under the §9.2.2.2.2 ban. §9.6.1 itself contains no restriction on where wait fork may appear.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix canvas page interactive bugs caused by inline component remounting

The CartContent was defined as a function component inside CanvasPage,
causing React to unmount/remount the entire cart DOM on every re-render.
This broke Select dropdowns (closing on mouse-out), Popover (customer
search closing on type), input focus loss, and button click handlers.

Fixes:
- Convert CartContent to renderCart() render function called as
  {renderCart()} so React reconciles it in the parent fiber tree
- Customer search: use CommandInput + shouldFilter=false instead of
  a raw input that bypassed cmdk selection handling
- Quantity input: replace static span with Input step=any to allow
  fractional quantities like 0.5 or 2.25
- Delivery/other fee inputs: use local string state so intermediate
  values like 150. are not wiped mid-typing
- Clear button: also resets discount type, fees, and fee string states

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: cache SSH output between discovery and reasoning phases (#2899) (#2942)

* feat: cache SSH output between discovery and reasoning phases (#2899)

Add TTL-based SSH output cache to SessionManager so repeated
capture_output() calls within the TTL window reuse cached results
instead of re-running SSH commands. Reduces SSH overhead during
the discovery→reasoning transition.

commit.message:
feat: expand agent guidance from Discord bot learnings (#19)

* docs: elevate progress.md to mandatory core instruction

progress.md was previously a recommendation — now it's a mandatory part of
every commit, on par with context.md. This eliminates the dual-source drift
problem where "Recent Changes" in context.md and progress.md entries told
competing stories.

Changes:
- Remove "Recent Changes" from context.md spec and template (history belongs
  in progress.md, not in a mutable snapshot)
- Strengthen Progress Log section: mandatory per-commit, archival policy at
  100 entries, quality guidance for descriptions
- Add progress.md entry check as item 9 in the self-review checklist
- Add archival note to progress.md template
- Update repo's own context.md and progress.md to match new spec

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add session-lifecycle, resource-awareness, and process-hygiene guidance

Distilled from iterating on the Discord bot (centralDiscord) development:

- guidance/session-lifecycle.md: session ephemerality, crash recovery
  mindset, multi-destination output design (terminal/Discord/WordPress)
- guidance/resource-awareness.md: discover-don't-memorize approach to
  server resources, concurrent job awareness, output size discipline
- guidance/process-hygiene.md: track spawned processes, atomic state
  writes, temp file cleanup, port conflict prevention

Also expanded agent.md:
- Deployment: concrete post-deploy verification protocol (pm2 show,
  curl health check, log scan) replacing vague "verify it's live"
- Debugging: logs-first as Step 0 before all other debugging steps
- Auto-Posting: multi-destination design section explaining viewport
  constraints and front-loading content for truncation resilience

Cross-references added from agent.md sections to new guidance files.
CLAUDE.md bootstrap loader updated with new guidance entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use venue timezone instead of UTC for date calculations

Vercel runs in UTC so new Date().toISOString() gives the wrong day
when it's past midnight UTC but still the same day in Chicago.
Added getLocalToday() helper using Intl.DateTimeFormat and applied
it to all admin and portal API routes. Also reseeded Supabase with
14 days of demo bookings, parties, check-ins, and waivers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Piano-layout answer grid: sharps/flats between natural notes

Switch from flex+spacers to a 13-column CSS grid.
Naturals sit at odd columns (1,3,5,7,9,11,13) and sharps/flats at even
columns (2,4,8,10,12), skipping column 6 (the E→F gap where no black key
exists). This mirrors a piano keyboard layout while keeping sharps on top
and flats on the bottom row.

commit.message:
Redesign execution API surface (#20)

## Summary

- Add `BaseRunnable` base class to `builders.py` — `Cmd`, `Fn`,
`Pipeline` inherit shared execution methods instead of each having their
own `start()`/`run()`/`out()` with local runtime imports
- Add typed `Result[OutT, ErrT]` namedtuple and `result()` method with 7
overloads for full capture control
- Redesign `out()` with `stdout`/`stderr`/`check`/`encoding` params and
12 overloads — replaces the old `out(encoding)` that only captured
stdout
- `run()` returns `None` and raises `ShishError` on non-zero exit (was:
returned `int` exit code)
- Add `code()` → `int`, `ok()` → `bool`, `err()` → `bool` convenience
methods
- Move `ShishError` from `runtime/api.py` to `builders.py`; remove
`run()`/`out()` from runtime (only `start()` remains)
- Add matching syntax-layer combinators: `result()`, `code()`, `ok()`,
`err()`, updated `out()`, `run()`
- `__await__` return type narrowed from `Generator[object, None, int]`
to `Generator[object]` (run returns None)
- Flatten overload tables with `# fmt: off`, ignore E501 in ruff
- Update README and CLAUDE.md for new API
- Move design docs to `docs/design/`

## Test plan

- [x] `just check` passes (format + pyright strict + 471 tests)
- [x] pyright resolves all `out()` overload combinations correctly
(str/bytes × stdout/stderr × check)
- [x] `out(stdout=False, stderr=False)` raises `ValueError`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hyperacceleration v5.2: Bitcoin-is-Time thesis + temporal economics

JARVIS knowledge: Gigi's "Bitcoin is Time" (PoW as decentralized clock,
tokens vs ledgers, double-spend as time problem, causality +
unpredictability = arrow of time in cyberspace), Lamport distributed
time ordering, VibeSwap batch auction as temporal MEV defense (eliminate
time-ordering within batch = eliminate MEV), CKB PoW anchoring to
physical reality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: filter observer sessions from deep-insights metrics

Observer/memory sessions (Claude-Mem) were counted as real work sessions,
skewing success_rate (40%→53%), inflating slightly_helpful (40%→22%),
and hiding that all 9 fully_achieved sessions were real work.

Filter uses text markers (observer|claude-mem|memory agent) in
brief_summary/underlying_goal + goal_categories markers. Does NOT use
warmup_minimal — proven to cause false positives on real data.

commit.message:
Fix snapshot not updating after insert

My previous refactor introduced a bug: deriving snapshot directly from
index meant that inserting while already at step 0 caused setIndex(0)
to be a no-op, so React skipped the re-render and the snapshot stayed
stale. Restored snapshot as useState, updated alongside step on every
navigation and insert. Also renamed index to step.

commit.message:
fix(renderer): full audit gap resolution — 9 pipeline fixes [step-9]

P0 — Prop contract fixes:
- Button: add label prop → renders label ?? children (blank CTAs fixed across all pages)
- Tabs: normalise string[] → Tab[]; accept tabsData[] for dynamic day tabs (agenda)
  (all 10+ status filter tab bars were rendering nothing)

P0 — Missing component:
- RendererSelect: styled <select> wired as Select in registry
  label + placeholder + options[] from optionsKey + current value from dataKey
  (event selector dropdowns on 8 pages were invisible in prod)

P1 — Data bridge:
- step2: multi-*Key injection — any prop ending in "Key" (except dataKey) resolves
  its value from ctx.data and injects as the stripped prop name
  e.g. activeDayKey:"activeDay" → activeDay: ctx.data["activeDay"]
  e.g. optionsKey:"events"      → options:   ctx.data["events"]
  e.g. tabsDataKey:"days"       → tabsData:  ctx.data["days"]
- 8 wireframes: added optionsKey:"events" to all select_input blocks

P1 — Status chip:
- RendererStatusChip: extracts status from entity object via statusKey prop,
  maps all 20+ domain status values to DL color tokens, replaces raw StatusChip
  (event_status blocks on Events[id] were rendering blank)

P2 — Dashboard wireframe cleanup:
- Removed top_navigation + breadcrumbs (duplicate of AppLayout)
- Moved page_header to top region, removed from primary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add 5-year backtest results (2020-2025, 600 central races)

V9 outperforms V8 consistently across all 6 years:
- V9 Trio: 33.7% hit rate, 91.1% ROI (vs V8: 22.2%, 63.1%)
- V9 Wide: 47.5% hit rate, 79.3% ROI (vs V8: 34.2%, 72.8%)
- V9 best year: 2024 (Trio 39%, ROI 102.2% - profitable)
- V9 2025: Trio 38%, ROI 116.2% - also profitable
- V9 Trio hit rate by field size: Small 67%, Medium 36%, Large 27%

commit.message:
fix: two bugs in GitHub Issues triage flow

1. create-issue: filter undefined labels before passing to GitHub API.
   bugLabelId/triageLabelId are Linear-specific and undefined when using
   GitHub Issues, causing a 422 Unprocessable Entity on issue creation.

2. findExistingPr: guard against empty searchTerm. When create-issue fails
   the issueIdentifier is empty string, causing regex /\b\b/ to match any
   PR title and incorrectly skip implementation.

commit.message:
Fix fail2ban binary detection using shutil.which for path-agnostic lookup v3.13.85

The hardcoded /usr/bin/fail2ban-client path check was not finding the binary
on some installations where it lives in /usr/local/bin or another location.
Use shutil.which() with explicit path fallbacks so detection works regardless
of install method or distro conventions.

ci: add GitHub Actions workflow for automated releases

Triggers on v* tag push. Builds Python engine (PyInstaller) + Tauri
NSIS installer on Windows runner, then publishes to GitHub Releases.
